title: Weather & Seasons
shortSummary: Talk about the weather and Korea's four distinct seasons in everyday Korean.
culturalNote: Korea has four sharply defined seasons, and they shape daily life and conversation. Spring (봄, bom) brings cherry blossoms and the famous 벚꽃 (beotkkot) viewing, while summer (여름, yeoreum) is hot, humid, and includes the rainy 장마 (jangma) monsoon period. Autumn (가을, gaeul) is crisp and prized for its red and gold foliage (단풍, danpung), and winter (겨울, gyeoul) is cold and dry with occasional heavy snow. Weather is common small talk, and Koreans often comment on the 미세먼지 (misemeonji, fine dust) air-quality levels, especially in spring, checking apps before going out. Seasonal foods and activities are taken seriously: 삼계탕 (samgyetang, ginseng chicken soup) in summer to build stamina, and warm street foods in winter. Commenting that the weather is 좋아요 (joayo, nice) or 추워요 (chuwoyo, cold) is a friendly, safe way to open a conversation.

grammar.explanation: Korean adjectives act like verbs and conjugate the same way. 덥다 (deopda, hot) becomes 더워요 (deowoyo, it's hot), and 춥다 (chupda, cold) becomes 추워요 (chuwoyo). No separate 'is' verb is needed.
grammar.commonMistake: Adding 이에요 after an adjective; adjectives already conjugate, so 추워요 needs no extra 'is'.

grammar.explanation: To say you like something, use 을/를 좋아해요 (joahaeyo). 봄 (bom, spring) becomes 봄을 좋아해요 (I like spring). The thing liked takes the object particle.
grammar.commonMistake: Confusing 좋아요 (it is good) with 좋아해요 (I like it); the second needs an object.

dialogue.setup: Two coworkers, Jihun and Claire, chat about the weather and seasons over coffee.

lines.speaker: 클레어
lines.english: It's a bit cold. The wind is blowing too.

lines.speaker: 지훈
lines.native: 겨울이니까요. 클레어 씨는 무슨 계절을 좋아해요?
lines.transliteration: gyeourinikkayo. keulleeo ssineun museun gyejeoreul joahaeyo?
lines.english: It's winter, after all. Which season do you like, Claire?

lines.speaker: 클레어
lines.native: 저는 가을이 제일 좋아요. 단풍이 예뻐요.
lines.transliteration: jeoneun gaeuri jeil joayo. danpungi yeppeoyo.
lines.english: I like autumn best. The foliage is pretty.

lines.speaker: 지훈
lines.english: Me too. Summer is too hot.

quiz.question: How do you ask 'How is the weather today?' in Korean?
quiz.explanation: 오늘 날씨가 어때요? (oneul nalssiga eottaeyo) asks about today's weather.

quiz.question: Which sentence means 'It's raining'?
quiz.explanation: 비가 와요 (biga wayo) means 'it's raining'; 눈이 와요 means 'it's snowing'.

quiz.question: 봄을 좋아_____. (I like spring)
quiz.explanation: 좋아해요 (joahaeyo) means 'like', so 봄을 좋아해요 means 'I like spring'.

quiz.question: Why do Koreans often check 미세먼지 levels?
options: Because of fine dust air quality
quiz.correctAnswer: Because of fine dust air quality
quiz.explanation: 미세먼지 (misemeonji) is fine dust; people check it, especially in spring, before going out.

quiz.question: Arrange: 좋아요 / 제일 / 가을이 (autumn is the best)
quiz.explanation: Subject, then 제일 (the most), then adjective: 가을이 제일 좋아요.
